perturbation.original_text: In the event that Oceanic Time Warner Cable removes the above content and or terminates this agreement prior to December 31, 2006, Oceanic Time Warner Cable agrees to pay a cancellation fee of Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000) per month multiplied by the number of months remain on the agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that Oceanic Time Warner Cable removes the above content and or terminates this agreement prior to December 31, 2006, Oceanic Time Warner Cable agrees to pay a cancellation fee. The amount of said cancellation fee will be determined at the discretion of Watchit Media.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specified a cancellation fee calculation of $5,000 per month. The modification removes this specific calculation and instead states the cancellation fee will be determined at the discretion of Watchit Media. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement initially laid out a fixed calculation, implying a mutual agreement on the fee structure. Now, changing to Watchit's discretion creates ambiguity and potential conflict if Oceanic Time Warner Cable disagrees with the determined amount. There is an uncertainty on how the fee is calculated, thus creating this in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that Oceanic Time Warner Cable removes the above content and or terminates this agreement prior to December 31, 2006, Oceanic Time Warner Cable agrees to pay a cancellation fee. The amount of said cancellation fee will be determined at the discretion of Watchit Media.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a clear calculation method for the cancellation fee ($5,000 per month). The changed text replaces this with a discretionary amount determined by Watchit Media. This directly contradicts the implicit agreement of a fixed calculation, introducing ambiguity and potential conflict, thus making it a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, however, the introduction of ambiguity to a previously fixed term creates a contradiction that falls under 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction', where essential language (the calculation) is removed, creating a contradiction with the implied obligation of a fixed fee.

perturbation.original_text: Watchit shall have the exclusive right to sell third party advertising as sponsors of their content and will have the right to brand the content under the Watchit brand and place a "bug" on the screen identifying the content with a Watchit trademark.
perturbation.changed_text: Watchit shall have the right to sell third party advertising as sponsors of their content. All branding decisions are subject to mutual agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stated Watchit has the exclusive right to sell third party advertising and the right to brand the content, including placing a "bug" with a trademark. The modified text removes the exclusivity on advertising rights and includes that branding decisions will now be subject to mutual agreement, which now contradicts the prior statement. Watchit branding is no longer unilaterally controlled by Watchit but is subjected to agreement. This omission creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Watchit shall have the right to sell third party advertising as sponsors of their content. All branding decisions are subject to mutual agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted Watchit the exclusive right to sell third-party advertising and unilateral control over branding, including placing a trademarked 'bug'. The changed text removes Watchit's exclusive advertising rights and subjects 'all branding decisions' to 'mutual agreement'. This directly contradicts the initial grant of unilateral branding control, creating a clear conflict regarding branding authority within the contract. The contradicted text reinforces this contradiction by reiterating the 'mutual agreement' for branding decisions while the original text suggested otherwise, creating an inconsistency that would cause confusion about which terms should be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: Ocean Time Warner Cable will provide Watchit with data on viewership to both Channel 777 and specifically to the content provided by Watchit.
perturbation.changed_text: Ocean Time Warner Cable will provide Watchit with data on viewership to Channel 777.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and specifically to the content provided by Watchit,' the modified text omits the detailed viewership data obligation. This omission contradicts the implicit understanding within the broader agreement that Watchit requires viewership data specifically for their content to assess its performance and attract advertisers. The contradiction lies in the fact that Watchit now only receives general viewership, and no viewership towards Watchit content. The in-text contradiction creates uncertainty regarding the value and measurement of the content provided, since the specific data is not being provided, hence creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ocean Time Warner Cable will provide Watchit with data on viewership to Channel 777.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that Ocean Time Warner Cable will provide Watchit with viewership data for Channel 777 and specifically for the content provided by Watchit. The changed text removes the specificity regarding Watchit's content, only obligating Ocean Time Warner Cable to provide viewership data for Channel 777 in general. This omission directly contradicts the implicit understanding that Watchit requires specific viewership data to measure content performance, hence the Contradicted Text provides data on general viewership. The changed text therefore weakens Watchit's ability to assess the value of their content. This creates a clear conflict, making the agreement inconsistent and potentially unenforceable with regard to Watchit's content-specific metrics, thus forming an in-text contradiction as per the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition provided. The altered section is in direct conflict with the contradicted text.
